claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T002_S052_PRE_02 ("I think we have time for actually a few questions. While people are thinking ...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T007_S009_FIR_02 ("So we may have many fewer documents for example 100,000 but all these documen...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "I think we have time for actually a few questions. While ..." is forced by the underlying constraint "So we may have many fewer documents for example 100,000 b..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S052) and the first-principle (snippet S009) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
